name: Stuffed Celery
recipeInstructions:
1. Cover bread in water and place in a small saucepan. Let soak for 5 minutes.
Drain excess water and cook over high heat, stirring until the bread becomes a paste,
similar to cooked cereal.
2. Separate celery stalks and remove stringy fibers. Cut into 36 pieces measuring
2 1/2 inches each. Place in a saucepan with 1 teaspoon salt. Add enough water to cover,
bring to a boil, and cook covered for 25 to 30 minutes. Drain and let cool.
3. In a bowl, combine cooked bread and meat. Add 1 egg, 1 tablespoon parsley, 1 teaspoon
salt, and 1/8 teaspoon pepper. Mix well to combine.
4. Divide the mixture into 18 small balls.
5. Place each ball between two pieces of celery and press so they hold together.
Roll in flour and place on wax paper. Repeat until all meat and celery have been used.
6. Beat remaining eggs with a pinch of salt. Heat 1/2 cup oil in a frying pan.
Dip stuffed celery in beaten eggs and fry until golden on all sides.
7. Place in a large skillet in a single layer. Add garlic, tomatoes, 3 tablespoons
oil, remaining parsley, and small amounts of salt and pepper. Cook over medium heat,
covered for 25 to 30 minutes. SERVES: 4-6
Note: Stuffed celery can be prepared ahead of time. Reheat briefly before serving;
it actually tastes better when served later.
